title: Add regression coverage for Markdown, HTML, and unknown text fallback
description:
Add regression coverage proving the new modular renderers do not break existing document-viewer behavior.

## Background

This feature expands the renderer surface area, so the final task should lock down behavior that already works: Markdown rendering, HTML rendering, and graceful fallback for text-like formats that do not get a specialized renderer.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] Tests prove Markdown rendering still supports the current heading, code-block, Mermaid, search-highlighting, and history behavior.
- [ ] Tests prove HTML rendering still routes through the iframe-based renderer with the current history suppression behavior.
- [ ] Tests prove unknown text-like formats still degrade gracefully instead of crashing or being misrouted.
- [ ] The final web-side test suite covering the new renderer matrix runs cleanly under the configured CI gate.

## Notes

This task lands after the renderer and viewer-shell changes so it can validate the final integrated behavior.
